001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.beans.*;
025import java.lang.annotation.*;
026import java.lang.reflect.*;
027import java.util.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029import java.util.stream.*;
030
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
034
035/**
036 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a Java method.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around {@link Method} that extends the standard Java reflection
040 * API with additional functionality for method introspection, annotation handling, and hierarchy traversal.
041 * It's designed to be lightweight, thread-safe, and cached for efficient reuse.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
044 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
045 *    <li>Method introspection - access method metadata, parameters, return type, exceptions
046 *    <li>Annotation support - get annotations from method and overridden methods in hierarchy
047 *    <li>Hierarchy traversal - find matching methods in parent classes and interfaces
048 *    <li>Type-safe access - wrapper around reflection with convenient methods
049 *    <li>Thread-safe - instances are immutable and safe for concurrent access
050 * </ul>
051 *
052 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
053 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
054 *    <li>Introspecting method metadata for code generation or analysis
055 *    <li>Finding annotations on methods including those from parent classes
056 *    <li>Discovering method hierarchies and overridden methods
057 *    <li>Working with method parameters and return types
058 *    <li>Building frameworks that need to analyze method signatures
059 * </ul>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
062 * <p class='bjava'>
063 *    <jc>// Get MethodInfo from a class</jc>
064 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
065 *    MethodInfo <jv>method</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
066 *
067 *    <jc>// Get return type</jc>
068 *    ClassInfo <jv>returnType</jv> = <jv>method</jv>.getReturnType();
069 *
070 *    <jc>// Get annotations including from parent methods</jc>
071 *    List&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> =
072 *       <jv>method</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).toList();
073 *
074 *    <jc>// Find matching methods in hierarchy</jc>
075 *    List&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>matching</jv> = <jv>method</jv>.getMatchingMethods();
076 * </p>
077 *
078 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
079 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo} - Class introspection
080 *    <li class='jc'>{@link FieldInfo} - Field introspection
081 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ConstructorInfo} - Constructor introspection
082 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ParameterInfo} - Parameter introspection
083 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
084 * </ul>
085 */
086@SuppressWarnings({
087   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
088})
089public class MethodInfo extends ExecutableInfo implements Comparable<MethodInfo>, Annotatable {
090
091   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
092   private static final String ARG_declaringClass = "declaringClass";
093   private static final String ARG_inner = "inner";
094   private static final String ARG_type = "type";
095
096   /**
097    * Creates a MethodInfo wrapper for the specified method.
098    *
099    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
100    * <p class='bjava'>
101    *    Method <jv>m</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
102    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = MethodInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>m</jv>);
103    * </p>
104    *
105    * @param declaringClass The class that declares this method. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
106    * @param inner The method being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
107    * @return A new MethodInfo object wrapping the method.
108    */
109   public static MethodInfo of(Class<?> declaringClass, Method inner) {
110      return ClassInfo.of(declaringClass).getMethod(inner);
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Creates a MethodInfo wrapper for the specified method.
115    *
116    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
117    * <p class='bjava'>
118    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
119    *    Method <jv>m</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
120    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = MethodInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>ci</jv>, <jv>m</jv>);
121    * </p>
122    *
123    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this method. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
124    * @param inner The method being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
125    * @return A new MethodInfo object wrapping the method.
126    */
127   public static MethodInfo of(ClassInfo declaringClass, Method inner) {
128      assertArgNotNull(ARG_declaringClass, declaringClass);
129      return declaringClass.getMethod(inner);
130   }
131
132   /**
133    * Creates a MethodInfo wrapper for the specified method.
134    *
135    * <p>
136    * This convenience method automatically determines the declaring class from the method.
137    *
138    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
139    * <p class='bjava'>
140    *    Method <jv>m</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getMethod(<js>"myMethod"</js>);
141    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = MethodInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>m</jv>);
142    * </p>
143    *
144    * @param inner The method being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
145    * @return A new MethodInfo object wrapping the method.
146    */
147   public static MethodInfo of(Method inner) {
148      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
149      return ClassInfo.of(inner.getDeclaringClass()).getMethod(inner);
150   }
151
152   private final Method inner;
153   private final Supplier<ClassInfo> returnType;
154   private final Supplier<List<MethodInfo>> matchingMethods;
155   private final Supplier<List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>>> annotations;
156
157   /**
158    * Constructor.
159    *
160    * <p>
161    * Creates a new MethodInfo wrapper for the specified method. This constructor is protected
162    * and should not be called directly. Use the static factory methods {@link #of(Method)} or
163    * obtain MethodInfo instances from {@link ClassInfo#getMethod(Method)}.
164    *
165    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this method.
166    * @param inner The method being wrapped.
167    */
168   protected MethodInfo(ClassInfo declaringClass, Method inner) {
169      super(declaringClass, inner);
170      this.inner = inner;
171      this.returnType = memoize(() -> ClassInfo.of(inner.getReturnType(), inner.getGenericReturnType()));
172      this.matchingMethods = memoize(this::findMatchingMethods);
173      this.annotations = memoize(() -> getMatchingMethods().stream().flatMap(m -> m.getDeclaredAnnotations().stream()).toList());
174   }
175
176   @Override /* Overridden from ExecutableInfo */
177   public MethodInfo accessible() {
178      super.accessible();
179      return this;
180   }
181
182   @Override
183   public int compareTo(MethodInfo o) {
184      int i = cmp(getNameSimple(), o.getNameSimple());
185      if (i == 0) {
186         var params = getParameters();
187         var oParams = o.getParameters();
188         i = params.size() - oParams.size();
189         if (i == 0) {
190            for (var j = 0; j < params.size() && i == 0; j++) {
191               i = cmp(params.get(j).getParameterType().getName(), oParams.get(j).getParameterType().getName());
192            }
193         }
194      }
195      // Tie-breaker: if methods have same name and parameters (e.g., overridden methods),
196      // prefer methods from child classes over parent classes
197      // This ensures consistent ordering and that child methods (with covariant return types) come first
198      if (i == 0) {
199         var declaringClass = getDeclaringClass();
200         var oDeclaringClass = o.getDeclaringClass();
201         if (declaringClass.isChildOf(oDeclaringClass)) {
202            i = -1; // This method is from a child class, so it comes first
203         } else if (oDeclaringClass.isChildOf(declaringClass)) {
204            i = 1; // Other method is from a child class, so it comes first
205         } else {
206            i = cmp(declaringClass.getName(), oDeclaringClass.getName()); // Neither is a child of the other, compare by class name for deterministic ordering
207         }
208      }
209      return i;
210   }
211
212   @Override /* Annotatable */
213   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.METHOD_TYPE; }
214
215   /**
216    * Returns an {@link AnnotatedType} object that represents the use of a type to specify the return type of the method.
217    *
218    * <p>
219    * Same as calling {@link Method#getAnnotatedReturnType()}.
220    *
221    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
222    * <p class='bjava'>
223    *    <jc>// For method: public @NotNull String getName()</jc>
224    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getMethod(<js>"getName"</js>);
225    *    AnnotatedType <jv>aType</jv> = <jv>mi</jv>.getAnnotatedReturnType();
226    *    <jc>// Check for @NotNull on the return type</jc>
227    * </p>
228    *
229    * @return An {@link AnnotatedType} object representing the return type.
230    * @see Method#getAnnotatedReturnType()
231    */
232   public AnnotatedType getAnnotatedReturnType() { return inner.getAnnotatedReturnType(); }
233
234   /**
235    * Returns all annotations on this method and parent overridden methods in child-to-parent order.
236    *
237    * <p>
238    * Results include annotations from:
239    * <ul>
240    *    <li>This method
241    *    <li>Matching methods in parent classes
242    *    <li>Matching methods in interfaces
243    * </ul>
244    *
245    * <p>
246    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
247    * Repeatable annotations (those marked with {@link java.lang.annotation.Repeatable @Repeatable}) are automatically
248    * expanded into their individual annotation instances. For example, if a method has multiple {@code @Marshalled} annotations,
249    * this method returns each {@code @Marshalled} annotation separately, rather than the container annotation.
250    *
251    * <p>
252    * List is unmodifiable.
253    *
254    * @return
255    *    A list of all annotations on this method and overridden methods.
256    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
257    */
258   public List<AnnotationInfo<Annotation>> getAnnotations() { return annotations.get(); }
259
260   /**
261    * Returns all annotations of the specified type on this method and parent overridden methods in child-to-parent order.
262    *
263    * <p>
264    * Results include annotations from:
265    * <ul>
266    *    <li>This method
267    *    <li>Matching methods in parent classes
268    *    <li>Matching methods in interfaces
269    * </ul>
270    *
271    * <p>
272    * <b>Note on Repeatable Annotations:</b>
273    * If the specified annotation type is repeatable (marked with {@link java.lang.annotation.Repeatable @Repeatable}),
274    * this method automatically expands container annotations into individual instances. This allows you to filter for
275    * a repeatable annotation and get back all individual occurrences without manually handling the container.
276    *
277    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
278    * <p class='bjava'>
279    *    <jc>// Get all @Marshalled annotations on this method and overridden methods</jc>
280    *    Stream&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;Bean&gt;&gt; <jv>beans</jv> = <jv>methodInfo</jv>.getAnnotations(Bean.<jk>class</jk>);
281    *    <jc>// If method has @Beans({@Marshalled(...), @Marshalled(...)}), both individual @Marshalled instances are returned</jc>
282    * </p>
283    *
284    * @param <A> The annotation type.
285    * @param type The annotation type to filter by.
286    * @return
287    *    A stream of matching annotation infos in child-to-parent order.
288    *    <br>Repeatable annotations are expanded into individual instances.
289    */
290   @SuppressWarnings({
291      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast for annotation stream
292   })
293   public <A extends Annotation> Stream<AnnotationInfo<A>> getAnnotations(Class<A> type) {
294      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
295      return getAnnotations().stream().filter(a -> a.isType(type)).map(a -> (AnnotationInfo<A>)a);
296   }
297
298   /**
299    * Returns the default value for the annotation member represented by this method.
300    *
301    * <p>
302    * Same as calling {@link Method#getDefaultValue()}.
303    *
304    * <p>
305    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if this method is not an annotation member, or if the annotation member has no default value.
306    *
307    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
308    * <p class='bjava'>
309    *    <jc>// For annotation: @interface MyAnnotation { String value() default "default"; }</jc>
310    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).getMethod(<js>"value"</js>);
311    *    Object <jv>defaultValue</jv> = <jv>mi</jv>.getDefaultValue();
312    *    <jc>// defaultValue is "default"</jc>
313    * </p>
314    *
315    * @return The default value, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
316    * @see Method#getDefaultValue()
317    */
318   public Object getDefaultValue() { return inner.getDefaultValue(); }
319
320   /**
321    * Returns a {@link Type} object that represents the formal return type of the method.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Same as calling {@link Method#getGenericReturnType()}.
325    *
326    * <p>
327    * If the return type is a parameterized type, the {@link Type} object returned reflects the actual type parameters used in the source code.
328    *
329    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
330    * <p class='bjava'>
331    *    <jc>// For method: public List&lt;String&gt; getValues()</jc>
332    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>).getMethod(<js>"getValues"</js>);
333    *    Type <jv>returnType</jv> = <jv>mi</jv>.getGenericReturnType();
334    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>returnType</jv> <jk>instanceof</jk> ParameterizedType) {
335    *       ParameterizedType <jv>pType</jv> = (ParameterizedType)<jv>returnType</jv>;
336    *       <jc>// pType.getActualTypeArguments()[0] is String.class</jc>
337    *    }
338    * </p>
339    *
340    * @return A {@link Type} object representing the formal return type.
341    * @see Method#getGenericReturnType()
342    */
343   public Type getGenericReturnType() { return inner.getGenericReturnType(); }
344
345   @Override /* Annotatable */
346   public String getLabel() { return getDeclaringClass().getNameSimple() + "." + getNameShort(); }
347
348   /**
349    * Returns this method and all matching methods up the hierarchy chain.
350    *
351    * <p>
352    * Searches parent classes and interfaces for methods with matching name and parameter types.
353    * Results are returned in the following order:
354    * <ol>
355    *    <li>This method
356    *    <li>Any matching methods on declared interfaces of this class
357    *    <li>Matching method on the parent class
358    *    <li>Any matching methods on the declared interfaces of the parent class
359    *    <li>Continue up the hierarchy
360    * </ol>
361    *
362    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
363    * <p class='bjava'>
364    *    <jc>// Interface and class hierarchy:</jc>
365    *    <jk>interface</jk> I1 {
366    *       <jk>void</jk> foo(String <jv>s</jv>);
367    *    }
368    *    <jk>class</jk> A {
369    *       <jk>void</jk> foo(String <jv>s</jv>) {}
370    *    }
371    *    <jk>interface</jk> I2 {
372    *       <jk>void</jk> foo(String <jv>s</jv>);
373    *    }
374    *    <jk>class</jk> B <jk>extends</jk> A <jk>implements</jk> I2 {
375    *       &#64;Override
376    *       <jk>void</jk> foo(String <jv>s</jv>) {}
377    *    }
378    *    <jc>// For B.foo(), returns: [B.foo, I2.foo, A.foo, I1.foo]</jc>
379    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ...;
380    *    List&lt;MethodInfo&gt; <jv>matching</jv> = <jv>mi</jv>.getMatchingMethods();
381    * </p>
382    *
383    * @return A list of matching methods including this one, in child-to-parent order.
384    */
385   public List<MethodInfo> getMatchingMethods() { return matchingMethods.get(); }
386
387   /**
388    * Returns the name of this method.
389    *
390    * @return The name of this method
391    */
392   public String getName() { return inner.getName(); }
393
394   /**
395    * Returns the bean property name if this is a getter or setter.
396    *
397    * @return The bean property name, or <jk>null</jk> if this isn't a getter or setter.
398    */
399   public String getPropertyName() {
400      String n = inner.getName();
401      if ((n.startsWith("get") || n.startsWith("set")) && n.length() > 3)
402         return Introspector.decapitalize(n.substring(3));
403      if (n.startsWith("is") && n.length() > 2)
404         return Introspector.decapitalize(n.substring(2));
405      return n;
406   }
407
408   /**
409    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method follows the Java Bean getter convention.
410    *
411    * <p>
412    * A getter is a public no-argument method whose name starts with {@code get} (with a non-void return type)
413    * or {@code is} (with a {@code boolean}/{@link Boolean} return type).
414    *
415    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method is a bean getter.
416    */
417   public boolean isGetter() {
418      var n = inner.getName();
419      if (getParameterCount() != 0)
420         return false;
421      if (n.startsWith("get") && n.length() > 3)
422         return !getReturnType().is(Void.TYPE);
423      if (n.startsWith("is") && n.length() > 2) {
424         var rt = getReturnType();
425         return rt.is(Boolean.TYPE) || rt.is(Boolean.class);
426      }
427      return false;
428   }
429
430   /**
431    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method follows the Java Bean setter convention.
432    *
433    * <p>
434    * A setter is a method whose name starts with {@code set} and accepts exactly one argument.
435    *
436    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method is a bean setter.
437    */
438   public boolean isSetter() {
439      var n = inner.getName();
440      return n.startsWith("set") && n.length() > 3 && getParameterCount() == 1;
441   }
442
443   /**
444    * Returns the generic return type of this method as a {@link ClassInfo} object.
445    *
446    * @return The generic return type of this method.
447    */
448   public ClassInfo getReturnType() { return returnType.get(); }
449
450   /**
451    * Returns the signature of this method.
452    *
453    * <p>
454    * For no-arg methods, the signature will be a simple string such as <js>"toString"</js>.
455    * For methods with one or more args, the arguments will be fully-qualified class names (e.g.
456    * <js>"append(java.util.StringBuilder,boolean)"</js>)
457    *
458    * @return The methods signature.
459    */
460   public String getSignature() {
461      var sb = new StringBuilder(128);
462      sb.append(inner.getName());
463      var params = getParameters();
464      if (!params.isEmpty()) {
465         sb.append('(');
466         for (var i = 0; i < params.size(); i++) {
467            if (i > 0)
468               sb.append(',');
469            params.get(i).getParameterType().appendNameFormatted(sb, ClassNameFormat.FULL, true, '$', ClassArrayFormat.BRACKETS);
470         }
471         sb.append(')');
472      }
473      return sb.toString();
474   }
475
476   /**
477    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has at least the specified parameters.
478    *
479    * <p>
480    * Method may or may not have additional parameters besides those specified.
481    *
482    * @param requiredParams The parameter types to check for.
483    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has at least the specified parameters.
484    */
485   public boolean hasAllParameters(Class<?>...requiredParams) {
486      var paramTypes = getParameters().stream().map(p -> p.getParameterType().inner()).toList();
487
488      for (var c : requiredParams)
489         if (! paramTypes.contains(c))
490            return false;
491
492      return true;
493   }
494
495   /**
496    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present on this method or any matching methods in parent classes/interfaces.
497    *
498    * <p>
499    * This method searches through all matching methods in the hierarchy.
500    *
501    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
502    * @param type The annotation to look for.
503    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified annotation is present on this method.
504    */
505   @Override
506   public <A extends Annotation> boolean hasAnnotation(Class<A> type) {
507      return getAnnotations(type).findAny().isPresent();
508   }
509
510   /**
511    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the parameters on the method only consist of the types specified in the list.
512    *
513    * <p>
514    * <b>Note:</b> This method is not meant to be used on methods with duplicate parameter types.
515    * It checks if each parameter type is present in the specified list, but does not verify
516    * that the count of each type matches exactly.
517    *
518    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
519    * <p class='bjava'>
520    *
521    *    <jc>// Example method:</jc>
522    *    <jk>public void</jk> foo(String <jv>bar</jv>, Integer <jv>baz</jv>);
523    *
524    *    <jv>fooMethod</jv>.hasOnlyParameterTypes(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// True.</jc>
525    *    <jv>fooMethod</jv>.hasOnlyParameterTypes(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, Map.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// True.</jc>
526    *    <jv>fooMethod</jv>.hasOnlyParameterTypes(String.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// False.</jc>
527    * </p>
528    *
529    * @param args The valid class types (exact) for the arguments.
530    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the method parameters only consist of the types specified in the list.
531    */
532   public boolean hasOnlyParameterTypes(Class<?>...args) {
533      for (var param : getParameters()) {
534         var c1 = param.getParameterType().inner();
535         var foundMatch = false;
536         for (var c2 : args)
537            if (c1 == c2)
538               foundMatch = true;
539         if (! foundMatch)
540            return false;
541      }
542      return true;
543   }
544
545   /**
546    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has the specified parameter.
547    *
548    * <p>
549    * Method may or may not have additional parameters besides the one specified.
550    *
551    * @param requiredParam The parameter type to check for.
552    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has at least the specified parameter.
553    */
554   public boolean hasParameter(Class<?> requiredParam) {
555      return hasAllParameters(requiredParam);
556   }
557
558   /**
559    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has a parameter that can accept the specified object.
560    *
561    * @param parameter The parameter object to check for.
562    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has at least one parameter that can accept the specified object.
563    */
564   public boolean hasParameter(Object parameter) {
565      return getParameters().stream().anyMatch(x -> x.canAccept(parameter));
566   }
567
568   /**
569    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has this return type.
570    *
571    * @param c The return type to test for.
572    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has this return type.
573    */
574   public boolean hasReturnType(Class<?> c) {
575      return inner.getReturnType() == c;
576   }
577
578   /**
579    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has this return type.
580    *
581    * @param ci The return type to test for.
582    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has this return type.
583    */
584   public boolean hasReturnType(ClassInfo ci) {
585      return hasReturnType(ci.inner());
586   }
587
588   /**
589    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has this parent return type.
590    *
591    * @param c The return type to test for.
592    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has this parent return type.
593    */
594   public boolean hasReturnTypeParent(Class<?> c) {
595      return ClassInfo.of(c).isAssignableFrom(inner.getReturnType());
596   }
597
598   /**
599    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method has this parent return type.
600    *
601    * @param ci The return type to test for.
602    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has this parent return type.
603    */
604   public boolean hasReturnTypeParent(ClassInfo ci) {
605      return hasReturnTypeParent(ci.inner());
606   }
607
608   /**
609    * Returns the wrapped method.
610    *
611    * @return The wrapped method.
612    */
613   public Method inner() {
614      return inner;
615   }
616
617   /**
618    * Compares this MethodInfo with the specified object for equality.
619    *
620    * <p>
621    * Two MethodInfo objects are considered equal if they wrap the same underlying {@link Method} object.
622    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Method#equals(Object)} method.
623    *
624    * <p>
625    * This method makes MethodInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
626    * and {@link HashSet}.
627    *
628    * @param obj The object to compare with.
629    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
630    */
631   @Override
632   public boolean equals(Object obj) {
633      return obj instanceof MethodInfo other && eq(this, other, (x, y) -> eq(x.inner, y.inner) && eq(x.declaringClass, y.declaringClass));
634   }
635
636   /**
637    * Returns a hash code value for this MethodInfo.
638    *
639    * <p>
640    * This combines the hash code of the underlying {@link Method} with the hash code of the declaring class
641    * to ensure that methods from different subclasses that inherit the same method are not considered equal.
642    *
643    * <p>
644    * This method makes MethodInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
645    * and {@link HashSet}.
646    *
647    * @return A hash code value for this MethodInfo.
648    */
649   @Override
650   public int hashCode() {
651      return 31 * inner.hashCode() + declaringClass.hashCode();
652   }
653
654   /**
655    * Shortcut for calling the invoke method on the underlying method.
656    *
657    * <p>
658    * If the underlying method is static, then the specified <c>obj</c> argument is ignored. It may be <jk>null</jk>.
659    *
660    * <p>
661    * If the number of formal parameters required by the underlying method is 0, the supplied <c>args</c> array may be of length 0 or <jk>null</jk>.
662    *
663    * @param <T> The method return type.
664    * @param obj The object the underlying method is invoked from.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for static methods.
665    * @param args The arguments used for the method call.  Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty for methods with no parameters.
666    * @return The object returned from the method.
667    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
668    */
669   @SuppressWarnings({
670      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T for method invocation result
671   })
672   public <T> T invoke(Object obj, Object...args) throws ExecutableException {
673      return safe(() -> {
674         try {
675            return (T)inner.invoke(obj, args);
676         } catch (InvocationTargetException e) {
677            throw exex(e.getTargetException());
678         }
679      }, e -> exex(e));
680   }
681
682   /**
683    * Invokes the specified method using lenient argument matching.
684    *
685    * <p>
686    * Lenient matching allows arguments to be matched to parameters based on parameter types.
687    * <br>Arguments can be in any order.
688    * <br>Extra arguments will be ignored.
689    * <br>Missing arguments will be left <jk>null</jk>.
690    *
691    * <p>
692    * Note that this only works for methods that have distinguishable argument types.
693    * <br>It's not going to work on methods with generic argument types like <c>Object</c>
694    *
695    * @param pojo
696    *    The POJO the method is being called on.
697    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> for static methods.
698    * @param args
699    *    The arguments to pass to the method.
700    * @return
701    *    The results of the method invocation.
702    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
703    */
704   public Object invokeLenient(Object pojo, Object...args) throws ExecutableException {
705      return safe((ThrowingSupplier<Object>)() -> inner.invoke(pojo, ClassUtils.getMatchingArgs(inner.getParameterTypes(), args)), e -> exex(e instanceof InvocationTargetException e2 ? e2.getTargetException() : e));
706   }
707
708   @Override
709   public boolean is(ElementFlag flag) {
710      return switch (flag) {
711         case BRIDGE -> isBridge();
712         case NOT_BRIDGE -> ! isBridge();
713         case DEFAULT -> isDefault();
714         case NOT_DEFAULT -> ! isDefault();
715         default -> super.is(flag);
716      };
717   }
718
719   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
720   // High Priority Methods (direct Method API compatibility)
721   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
722
723   /**
724    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method is a bridge method.
725    *
726    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method is a bridge method.
727    */
728   public boolean isBridge() { return inner.isBridge(); }
729
730   /**
731    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method is a default method (Java 8+ interface default method).
732    *
733    * <p>
734    * Same as calling {@link Method#isDefault()}.
735    *
736    * <p>
737    * A default method is a public non-abstract instance method (i.e., non-static method with a body) declared in an interface.
738    *
739    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
740    * <p class='bjava'>
741    *    <jc>// For interface: interface MyInterface { default String getName() { return "default"; } }</jc>
742    *    MethodInfo <jv>mi</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyInterface.<jk>class</jk>).getMethod(<js>"getName"</js>);
743    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>mi</jv>.isDefault()) {
744    *       <jc>// This is a default interface method</jc>
745    *    }
746    * </p>
747    *
748    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method is a default method.
749    * @see Method#isDefault()
750    */
751   public boolean isDefault() { return inner.isDefault(); }
752
753   /**
754    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this method matches the specified method by name and parameter types.
755    *
756    * @param m The method to compare against.
757    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this method has the same name and parameter types as the specified method.
758    */
759   public boolean matches(MethodInfo m) {
760      return hasName(m.getName()) && hasMatchingParameters(m.getParameters());
761   }
762
763   private void addMatchingMethodsFromInterface(List<MethodInfo> result, ClassInfo iface) {
764      // Add matching methods from this interface
765      iface.getDeclaredMethods().stream().filter(this::matches).forEach(result::add);
766
767      // Recursively search parent interfaces
768      iface.getDeclaredInterfaces().forEach(pi -> addMatchingMethodsFromInterface(result, pi));
769   }
770
771   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
772   // Annotatable interface methods
773   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
774
775   private List<MethodInfo> findMatchingMethods() {
776      var result = new ArrayList<MethodInfo>();
777      result.add(this); // 1. This method
778
779      var cc = getDeclaringClass();
780
781      while (nn(cc)) {
782         // 2. Add matching methods from declared interfaces of current class
783         cc.getDeclaredInterfaces().forEach(di -> addMatchingMethodsFromInterface(result, di));
784
785         // 3. Move to parent class
786         cc = cc.getSuperclass();
787         if (nn(cc)) {
788            // Add matching method from parent class
789            cc.getDeclaredMethods().stream().filter(this::matches).findFirst().ifPresent(result::add);
790         }
791      }
792
793      return result;
794   }
795
796   /**
797    * Returns comma-delimited list of missing parameter types.
798    *
799    * <p>
800    * Analyzes the parameters of this method and checks if all required beans are available
801    * in the bean store or in the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
802    *
803    * <p>
804    * The following parameter types are considered optional and are not checked:
805    * <ul>
806    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
807    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
808    * </ul>
809    *
810    * <p>
811    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja> or <ja>@Qualifier</ja> annotation,
812    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
813    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
814    *
815    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
816    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
817    *    These are checked after the bean store but before marking the parameter as missing.
818    * @return A comma-delimited, sorted list of missing parameter types (e.g., <js>"String,Integer"</js>),
819    *    or <jk>null</jk> if all required parameters are available.
820    */
821   public String getMissingParameterTypes(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
822      // @formatter:off
823      return nullIfEmpty(
824         getParameters()
825            .stream()
826            .map(x -> x.getMissingType(beanStore, otherBeans))
827            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
828            .sorted()
829            .collect(joining(","))
830      );
831      // @formatter:on
832   }
833
834   /**
835    * Checks if all method parameters can be resolved.
836    *
837    * <p>
838    * This method performs the same checks as {@link #getMissingParameterTypes(BeanStore, Object...)} but
839    * returns a boolean instead of a list of missing types.
840    *
841    * <p>
842    * The following parameter types are considered optional and are not checked:
843    * <ul>
844    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
845    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
846    * </ul>
847    *
848    * <p>
849    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja> or <ja>@Qualifier</ja> annotation,
850    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
851    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
852    *
853    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
854    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
855    *    These are checked after the bean store but before marking the parameter as missing.
856    * @return <jk>true</jk> if all required parameters are available in the bean store or <c>otherBeans</c>, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
857    */
858   public boolean canResolveAllParameters(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
859      return getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.canResolve(beanStore, otherBeans)).allMatch(x -> x);
860   }
861
862   /**
863    * Resolves parameters from the bean store and invokes the method.
864    *
865    * <p>
866    * For each parameter in the method, this method:
867    * <ul>
868    *    <li>If the parameter is a collection/array/map type, collects all beans of the element type.
869    *    <li>Otherwise, looks up a single bean by type and optional qualifier name in the bean store.
870    *    <li>If not found in the bean store, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
871    * </ul>
872    *
873    * <h5 class='section'>Parameter Resolution:</h5>
874    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
875    *    <li><b>Single beans</b> - Resolved using {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class)} or {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class, String)}.
876    *       If not found, checks <c>otherBeans</c> for a compatible instance.
877    *       Throws {@link ExecutableException} if not found in either location.
878    *    <li><b>Optional beans</b> - Wrapped in <c>Optional</c>, or <c>Optional.empty()</c> if not found.
879    *       Never throws an exception.
880    *    <li><b>Arrays</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into an array (may be empty).
881    *       Never throws an exception.
882    *    <li><b>Lists</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>List</c> (may be empty).
883    *       Never throws an exception.
884    *    <li><b>Sets</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashSet</c> (may be empty).
885    *       Never throws an exception.
886    *    <li><b>Maps</b> - All beans of the value type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashMap</c> keyed by bean name (may be empty).
887    *       Unnamed beans use an empty string as the key.  Never throws an exception.
888    * </ul>
889    *
890    * <p>
891    * The <c>bean</c> parameter is the object instance on which to invoke the method (or <jk>null</jk> for static methods).
892    *
893    * @param <T> The return type of the method.
894    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve parameters from.
895    * @param bean The object instance on which to invoke the method (or <jk>null</jk> for static methods).
896    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
897    * @return The result of invoking the method.
898    * @throws ExecutableException If the method cannot be invoked or parameter resolution fails.
899    */
900   public <T> T inject(BeanStore beanStore, Object bean, Object... otherBeans) {
901      var params = getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.resolveValue(beanStore, otherBeans)).toArray();
902      return accessible().invoke(bean, params);
903   }
904
905   /**
906    * Resolves all method parameters from the bean store.
907    *
908    * <p>
909    * This is a package-private helper method used by tests.  For normal usage, use {@link #inject(BeanStore, Object, Object...)}.
910    *
911    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve beans from.
912    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
913    * @return An array of parameter values in the same order as the method parameters.
914    * @throws ExecutableException If a required parameter cannot be resolved.
915    */
916   Object[] resolveParameters(BeanStore beanStore, Object... otherBeans) {
917      return getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.resolveValue(beanStore, otherBeans)).toArray();
918   }
919}
